  "title": "Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics",
  "description": "M-KOPA is a fast-growing FinTech company offering millions of underbanked customers across Africa access to life-enhancing products and services. From our roots as the pioneer in pay-as-you-go &ldquo;PayGo&rsquo;&rdquo; solar energy for off grid homes, we have grown into one of the most advanced connected asset financing platforms in the world, empowering a broad range of customers to achieve progress in their lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time

About the Job


	We are looking for a Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics to join our Warehouse team in Lagos, Nigeria where progress, and humility are valued as highly as technical excellence.&nbsp;


The Role


	As a Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics, you&#39;re the first line of quality control in our device returns pipeline.&nbsp;
	You receive, diagnose, classify, and hand over returned devices with the rigour and pace that keeps downstream operations - refurbishment, resale, write-off; running on time and with confidence.&nbsp;
	Your work directly determines what gets recovered and what gets lost.


What You&#39;ll Do


	Receive, log, and process incoming devices daily - hitting the 150&ndash;170 device receipting target while executing the mandatory pre-check protocol: power-on status, physical damage, screen functionality, IMEI validation, and FRP/password lock screening.&nbsp;
	Manage device turnaround with precision: every unit moves from warehouse receipting to the Refurbs queue within 48 hours, with all five device categories tracked to a 24&ndash;72 hour TAT standard and a 95%+ quality reliability rate maintained across the pipeline.&nbsp;
	Drive backlog reduction, prepare verified batch handovers for the weekly Monday Refurbs handover, and keep daily pre-check logs accurate and current to feed real-time reporting.&nbsp;


What You&#39;ll Need


	Demonstrable hands-on experience in device inspection, mobile phone diagnostics, or electronics quality control with a track record of working accurately at volume.&nbsp;
	Proven ability to manage structured workflows under strict turnaround standards, with attention to detail that holds even when throughput demands are high.&nbsp;
	Experience working with inventory or asset tracking systems, and comfort updating operational logs that feed into live reporting dashboards.&nbsp;


The honest part:


	This role moves fast and the standards are unambiguous.
	You&#39;ll be expected to hit daily targets, clear a significant inherited backlog, and maintain quality rates that protect the downstream refurbishment pipeline.&nbsp;
	If you thrive on measurable output and take satisfaction in knowing exactly where you stand at the end of each day, this is your kind of environment.

Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics Job at M-KOPA Nigeria




        
    

        
        M-KOPA is a fast-growing FinTech company offering millions of underbanked customers across Africa access to life-enhancing products and services. From our roots as the pioneer in pay-as-you-go “PayGo’” solar energy for off grid homes, we have grown into one of the most advanced connected asset financing platforms in the world, empowering a broad range of customers to achieve progress in their lives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time

About the Job
     

            
    




	We are looking for a Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics to join our Warehouse team in Lagos, Nigeria where progress, and humility are valued as highly as technical excellence. 


The Role


	As a Warehouse Assistant - Reverse Logistics, you're the first line of quality control in our device returns pipeline. 
	You receive, diagnose, classify, and hand over returned devices with the rigour and pace that keeps downstream operations - refurbishment, resale, write-off; running on time and with confidence. 
	Your work directly determines what gets recovered and what gets lost.


What You'll Do


	Receive, log, and process incoming devices daily - hitting the 150–170 device receipting target while executing the mandatory pre-check protocol: power-on status, physical damage, screen functionality, IMEI validation, and FRP/password lock screening. 
	Manage device turnaround with precision: every unit moves from warehouse receipting to the Refurbs queue within 48 hours, with all five device categories tracked to a 24–72 hour TAT standard and a 95%+ quality reliability rate maintained across the pipeline. 
	Drive backlog reduction, prepare verified batch handovers for the weekly Monday Refurbs handover, and keep daily pre-check logs accurate and current to feed real-time reporting. 


What You'll Need


	Demonstrable hands-on experience in device inspection, mobile phone diagnostics, or electronics quality control with a track record of working accurately at volume. 
	Proven ability to manage structured workflows under strict turnaround standards, with attention to detail that holds even when throughput demands are high. 
	Experience working with inventory or asset tracking systems, and comfort updating operational logs that feed into live reporting dashboards. 


The honest part:


	This role moves fast and the standards are unambiguous.
	You'll be expected to hit daily targets, clear a significant inherited backlog, and maintain quality rates that protect the downstream refurbishment pipeline. 
	If you thrive on measurable output and take satisfaction in knowing exactly where you stand at the end of each day, this is your kind of environment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Mechanical Engineer Job at Mudiame University




        
    

        
        Mudiame University is a privately-owned University in Irrua, Edo State, Nigeria. The institution has been officially accredited and/or recognized by the National Universities Commission (NUC), Nigeria, and is on a mission to contribute to the sustainable welfare of society with expertise in science, technology and business, and through teaching, research and community service.

Mudiame University has the vision to be a world-leading institution for technological innovation that addresses the challenges of agriculture and food safety, energy, health, manufacturing, oil and gas, security and transportation on a regional and global scale.

Applications are invited from suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:
     

            
    



Job Position: Mechanical Engineer

Job Location: Irrua, Edo
Department: Works and Physical Planning 

Requirements


	Good honours Degree – HND or BSc with a minimum of Second Class Lower Division in a relevant field from a recognized institution 
	Possession of NYSC Discharge/Exemption/Exclusion Certificate.
	Registered/certified member of a relevant professional body with a practicing License.
	Possession of a higher degree will be of added advantage
	Computer Literacy is compulsory, except Artisans. Interviews will include Computer-Based Tests.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The Role


	We are seeking a highly organized and experienced Male Administrative Officer to join our growing transport and logistics company.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong background in administration within a transport, logistics, or fleet-based environment and possess excellent analytical, reporting, and operational support skills.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage daily administrative operations and ensure efficient office procedures.
	Prepare and analyze reports, presentations, and dashboards using Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Power BI.
	Utilize AI tools to improve productivity, reporting, data analysis, and process efficiency.
	Coordinate and monitor fleet management activities, including vehicle documentation, maintenance schedules, and compliance requirements.
	Handle and follow up on insurance claims related to company vehicles and assets.
	Oversee facility management, ensuring office and operational facilities are properly maintained and functional.
	Maintain accurate records, databases, and administrative documentation.
	Support management with business reporting, budgeting, and operational analysis.
	Liaise with vendors, service providers, insurance companies, and regulatory agencies.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Transport Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in Administration, preferably within the transport, logistics, or fleet management sector.
	Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Power BI.
	Demonstrated ability to effectively use AI-powered tools for business productivity and reporting.
	Solid understanding of Fleet Management, vehicle documentation, maintenance tracking, and operational support.
	Experience managing Insurance Claims and coordinating with insurance providers.
	Knowledge of Facility Management and office administration best practices.
	Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.


Preferred Competencies:


	Data analysis and reporting expertise.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy.
	Strong stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Proactive and results-oriented mindset.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Administrative Officer Job at Vodstra Limited




        
    

        
        Vodstra Limited is a corporate organization whose mission is to use disruptive and innovative strategies to help SMEs in Nigeria grow their leadership, business, and entrepreneurship. We assist local and international organizations by providing business solutions and expert advice to enable organizations to develop the skills and competencies needed to thrive and survive in the world's challenging and growing economies.

Our team of experts has several years of combined experience in improving organizational capability through Leadership Development, Coaching, Talent Management, Business Advisory, HR Consultancy, Risk Management, and Legal Services. We also assist organizations in developing strategies that position them to compete favorably with their industry peers.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The Role


	We are seeking a highly organized and experienced Male Administrative Officer to join our growing transport and logistics company.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong background in administration within a transport, logistics, or fleet-based environment and possess excellent analytical, reporting, and operational support skills.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage daily administrative operations and ensure efficient office procedures.
	Prepare and analyze reports, presentations, and dashboards using Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Power BI.
	Utilize AI tools to improve productivity, reporting, data analysis, and process efficiency.
	Coordinate and monitor fleet management activities, including vehicle documentation, maintenance schedules, and compliance requirements.
	Handle and follow up on insurance claims related to company vehicles and assets.
	Oversee facility management, ensuring office and operational facilities are properly maintained and functional.
	Maintain accurate records, databases, and administrative documentation.
	Support management with business reporting, budgeting, and operational analysis.
	Liaise with vendors, service providers, insurance companies, and regulatory agencies.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and industry regulations.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Logistics, Transport Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in Administration, preferably within the transport, logistics, or fleet management sector.
	Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Power BI.
	Demonstrated ability to effectively use AI-powered tools for business productivity and reporting.
	Solid understanding of Fleet Management, vehicle documentation, maintenance tracking, and operational support.
	Experience managing Insurance Claims and coordinating with insurance providers.
	Knowledge of Facility Management and office administration best practices.
	Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
	Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.


Preferred Competencies:


	Data analysis and reporting expertise.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy.
	Strong stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Proactive and results-oriented mindset.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected], using the Job Position as the mail subject
